entries.reason: Single Responsibility — Draft focuses on one concern: auto-creating missing board status options to prevent workflow command failures.; Unambiguous Requirements — Uses non-verifiable wording like 「明確なエラーメッセージ」 without measurable acceptance criteria.; Complete Context — Core requirements consistently define conditions (missing status/field) and expected behavior (auto-create or fail with error).; No Hardcoded Secrets — No API keys, tokens, passwords, or embedded secrets appear in the draft.; Prioritize Requirements — More than three requirements are present, but no explicit priority order is defined.; Impact on Existing Features — Includes a dedicated impact section and explicitly states unchanged behavior for existing boards.; Present Recommendations with Reasoning — Q&A provides answers, but does not state concise recommendation basis from docs/guardrails/code patterns for each question.; Explore Before Asking — No unresolved user-directed questions remain, and code locations were already investigated.; Proactively Raise Related Implications — Draft proactively covers edge cases, concurrency failure behavior, and scope boundaries.; Confirm Brainstorm vs. Decision Before Critiquing — Inapplicable; content is already a finalized/approved draft, not critique of brainstorming ideas.; Capture Digressions in draft.md — Inapplicable; no digression thread is present in the provided content.; Draft Stays at Requirements Level — Includes implementation-level details (specific file paths, mutation placement, export/signature checks).; Backward — Compatible CLI Interface — No existing command/option is removed or redefined, so migration planning is not required.

Single Responsibility — The draft focuses on one concern: auto-creating missing board status options to prevent command failure.; Unambiguous Requirements — Requirements are mostly stated as verifiable outcomes (e.g., success/fail behavior, extra call conditions, explicit error guidance).; Complete Context — Requirements pair conditions (missing status/field states) with expected behavior (auto-create or stop with error).; No Hardcoded Secrets — No API keys, passwords, tokens, or secret literals are present; this article is inapplicable to the draft text.; Prioritize Requirements — Four requirements are explicitly ordered by priority (high/medium/low).; Impact on Existing Features — A dedicated impact section states affected and unaffected behavior explicitly.; Present Recommendations with Reasoning — Each Q&A answer leads with a recommendation and includes rationale grounded in issue context, guardrails, or existing code patterns.; Explore Before Asking — The draft cites specific files/tests and leaves no unresolved question that should have been answered by prior exploration.; Proactively Raise Related Implications — It proactively addresses edge cases, failure modes, and future extensibility implications.; Confirm Brainstorm vs. Decision Before Critiquing — The draft is presented as an approved decision, not a brainstorming critique scenario, so this is inapplicable.; Capture Digressions in draft.md — No digression is evident; the content remains on-topic in the draft itself.; Draft Stays at Requirements Level — It includes implementation-level details (specific file paths, GraphQL path/mutation handling, internal return structure notes).; Backward — Compatible CLI Interface — No existing command/option removal or semantic change is proposed, so a migration plan is not required.

Single Responsibility — The draft focuses on one concern: preventing `status not found` failures by auto-creating missing status options.; Unambiguous Requirements — Some requirement wording is not fully verifiable (e.g., “安全に停止”, “挙動が変わらない”) without explicit measurable criteria.; Complete Context — Each requirement includes a condition context (e.g., missing status/field, existing board state) and expected behavior.; No Hardcoded Secrets — The content contains no API keys, tokens, passwords, or other embedded secrets.; Prioritize Requirements — There are four requirements and they are explicitly ordered by priority (高/中/低).; Impact on Existing Features — It explicitly states both unchanged behavior on existing boards and added API calls on new boards.; Present Recommendations with Reasoning — Each Q&A item gives a clear recommended answer plus concise rationale tied to issue intent, guardrails, or existing patterns.; Explore Before Asking — No unresolved user-directed questions remain, and “Open Questions: なし” indicates no avoidable asks were deferred.; Proactively Raise Related Implications — The draft proactively covers edge cases, failure modes, concurrency behavior, and future extension implications.; Confirm Brainstorm vs. Decision Before Critiquing — Inapplicable here because this draft is presented as an approved decision, not an active critique of brainstorming.; Capture Digressions in draft.md — Inapplicable here because no digressive discussion is present in the provided content.; Draft Stays at Requirements Level — The document stays at scope/requirements/test-strategy level and avoids function-level or code-level implementation design.; Backward — Compatible CLI Interface — Inapplicable because no existing command/option is removed or semantically changed, so no migration plan is required.

line 10: unresolved token (`experimental/workflow` の `add` / `update` / `publish` コマンドが、GitHub Projects ボードに必要な status (`Ideas` / `Todo` 等) が事前に存在しない場合に `status not found` で失敗する問題を解消する。新規ボードでも追加セットアップなしでボード操作 CLI が動作する状態にする。); line 15: unresolved token (- `publish` 実行時、`Todo` status が存在しなければ自動作成してから処理を続行する); line 65: unresolved token (- 新規 ProjectV2 ボード（Status フィールドのみ存在し option が無い、または `Ideas`/`Todo` が無い）に対し、`add` / `publish` を実行すると、必要な status option が自動作成され、コマンドが exit code 0 で完了する。); line 66: unresolved token (- 既に `Ideas` / `Todo` が存在するボードで `add` / `publish` を実行した場合、option 作成のための GraphQL 呼び出しは行われず、現行と同じ結果（item 作成 / issue 化）が得られる。); line 85: unchecked task/question (- [ ] なし)
line 17: unresolved token (以降、本文では「draft 用 status」「publish 後 status」と呼称する（本文中で `Todo` をリテラル単語として書くと spec gate の `\bTODO\b` マーカー検知に当たるため、リテラルは上記テーブル内に限定する）。)

status not — found エラーの解消（add/update/publish で不足 status を自動作成して継続） — `ensureStatusOption` を `add`/`update`/`publish` で使用し、未存在時は `addSingleSelectOption` で作成後に `setItemStatus` を実行する実装に置換されている。; Status フィールド自体が無い場合の安全な停止 — `ensureStatusOption` が `meta.statusField` 不在時に即時 `Error` を投げ、option 作成 mutation を呼ばずに失敗終了する。; 既存ボードへの非影響（既存 status では option 作成 GraphQL 0回） — `ensureStatusOption` は既存 option 検出時に `addSingleSelectOption` を呼ばず既存 `optionId` を返す分岐になっている。; Status フィールド欠如時のエラー案内 — 例外文言に `"Status"` フィールド名と `SingleSelectField` 必須である旨が明記されている。; No Hardcoded Secrets — Diff and spec contain no API keys, passwords, tokens, or other embedded secrets.; No Silent Error Swallowing — No empty `catch` blocks or silent error drops are shown; failures throw explicit errors.; Bounded Resource Usage — `addSingleSelectOption` reads and rewrites the full options list with no explicit upper bound on option count.; No Sensitive Data in Logs — Added errors/messages reference only field and status names, not secrets or PII.; No Disabling Existing Tests — No test skipping, deletion, or commenting-out is shown in the provided changes.; Escalate When New Tests Break Existing Tests — Inapplicable: no evidence of test execution results or breakages in the provided content.; Do Not Modify Existing Tests Without Approval — No existing test files are modified or deleted in the shown diff.; Flag Tests That Become Irrelevant — Inapplicable: no explicit obsolete-test scenario or test refactor is shown in the provided content.; Exit Code Contract — Failure paths throw errors (e.g., missing `Status` field), which aligns with non-zero exit behavior.; No Synchronous I/O in Hot Paths — No new synchronous I/O is introduced inside loops or bulk-processing paths.; Validate User Input at Entry Point — `--status`/status names are used for option creation without visible entry-point validation constraints.
